records.ety: [F. vinent, l. evidens, -entis; e out + videns, p. pr. of videre to see. See Vision.]

defs: Clear to the vision; especially, clear to the understanding, and satisfactory to the judgment; as, the figure or color of a body is evident to the senses; the guilt of an offender can not always be made evident.
quotes: Your honor and your goodness is so evident.
And in our faces evident the signs Of foul concupiscence.
